name: Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando
mission: The mission of the Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando is to provide quality education of the highest standard in a safe, supportive, dynamic, and engaging Islamic environment so that students have the opportunity to excel to their fullest potential.
theoryOfChange: By providing a dynamic student-centered educational environment with high academic standards and expectations, Islamic values and morals, students will develop a sense of purpose, a connection to ALLAH, and a desire to serve their creator via service to others and their community.
scoreSummary: Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando Inc shows moderate alignment and moderate impact (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Top 20% of Private Schools in Florida (Private School Review)

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions a lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data and a program expense ratio of 67.8% as contributing to the impact score of 22/50. However, it fails to mention the significant deduction due to the weak governance (board of 1 member) which is a key factor in the low impact score.
Website citation for 'claims_zakat_eligible' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Board too small: 1 members

score_details.score_summary: Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando Inc shows moderate alignment and moderate impact (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: No explicit mention of accepting zakat donations was found on the Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando's website or in related search results. While other Islamic organizations in Orlando mention zakat, and one snippet mentions 'Support our Islamic school with your Zakat contributions', the latter refers to 'Ummul Qura Islamic Foundation' and not directly to the Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando. (Source: https://magorlando.net)

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 24/50, the organization serves a critical need in global education but shows a lower fit for donors seeking high-directness interventions [6][8]. As a sadaqah-eligible institution, it provides a consistent track record for general charitable giving within the Florida educational landscape [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The academy focuses on capacity building through its Pre-K and KG-Grade 8 programs, though its impact score of 22/50 reflects a lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data [1][3]. While the organization maintains a healthy 15.6 months of working capital, only 67.8% of its $3,145,617 total revenue is directed toward program expenses [1][2].

baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando (MAGO) is a non-profit educational institution dedicated to providing quality schooling for children from Pre-K through 8th grade [3][4]. With a mission focused on fostering virtue and wisdom, the academy integrates academic excellence with character development [5][7].

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization does not currently meet BBB standards due to its <cite id="15">limited board size</cite> and lack of public governance documentation.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The school's long operating history since 2006 and high academic ratings provide some mitigation against governance concerns.
case_against.summary: The primary risk factor is the <cite id="15">minimal board oversight</cite>, which creates a single point of failure for governance.

confidence.notes: Financial data is strong, but governance and impact transparency are limited.

alignment.improvement: The school could improve alignment by <cite id="15">clearly stating its sadaqah policies</cite> or expanding services to more underserved populations.

credibility.explanation: While the academy is a <cite id="1">registered 501(c)(3)</cite> with a <cite id="14">20-year track record</cite>, credibility is hampered by <cite id="15">weak board governance</cite> and a lack of third-party transparency seals.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the academy should <cite id="15">expand its board of directors</cite> to include more independent members and publish annual impact reports.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando exhibits strong fiscal management in terms of liquidity and growth. With <cite id="1">total assets of $3,270,864</cite> and a <cite id="12">revenue growth rate of 25.5%</cite>, the school is in a phase of rapid expansion. This growth is supported by a <cite id="1">67.8% program expense ratio</cite> reported in recent filings, indicating that all functional expenses are directed toward the educational mission. Furthermore, the school has clear <cite id="4">admission requirements for KG-Grade 8</cite> and <cite id="5">Pre-K</cite>, ensuring a structured environment for its student body.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando (MAGO) is an established educational institution that has been <cite id="1">serving the community since 2006</cite>. The academy provides a comprehensive faith-based curriculum spanning <cite id="3">Pre-K through 8th grade</cite>, focusing on academic excellence integrated with Islamic values. Financially, the organization demonstrated significant growth with <cite id="2">total revenue reaching $3,572,587</cite> in the 2024 tax year, representing a <cite id="12">three-year revenue CAGR of 25.5%</cite>. While the organization maintains <cite id="13">exceptional fundraising efficiency at $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, its operational model relies on a mix of <cite id="1">$3,093,110 in contributions</cite> and <cite id="1">$479,477 in program service revenue</cite>. The academy is recognized for its quality, ranking in the <cite id="14">top 20% of private schools in Florida</cite> and maintaining an <cite id="14">Overall Niche Grade of A+</cite>. Despite these academic successes, the organization's governance structure is currently a point of concern, as it operates with a <cite id="15">board size of only 1 member</cite>, which impacts its overall transparency and accountability metrics.
